text.title: FinOps — Reserved Instances / Savings Plans Commitment Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team committed to peak Black-Friday capacity; utilization averages 40% the other 11 months; unused commitment burns ~$200k/yr.
antipattern.root-cause: Author sized the commitment to absolute peak rather than baseline.
antipattern.fix: Recompute commitment against 3-month p25 utilization; cover only base; let peak run on-demand.

antipattern.symptom: Standard RI bought for m5.large; team migrates to Graviton (m6g) 4 months later; RI sits unused for 32 months.
antipattern.root-cause: Author chose Standard for the 3pp extra discount without modeling workload-change risk.
antipattern.fix: Prefer Compute Savings Plan or Convertible RI when migration is plausible; the flexibility premium pays for itself on the first family swap.

antipattern.symptom: All 12 RIs expire same week; team caught flat-footed; on-demand spike doubles bill for 2 months.
antipattern.root-cause: All commitments purchased in a single procurement cycle.
antipattern.fix: Stagger renewals: split commitments across quarters; queue renewals 60 days in advance with `fco-cost-allocation` reports as evidence.
